title: Regulated Virtual Assets Activities are now offered at DET branches
summary: On 29 May 2023, VARA -- the Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority, which regulates virtual assets in Dubai outside the DIFC free zone -- and Dubai's Department of Economy and Tourism (DET) notified the market that the Regulated Virtual Assets Activities VARA had issued on 7 February 2023 are now available to apply for at physical DET branches, including Al Barsha Mall, DED Cafe, Palm Strip Mall Jumeirah, Al Twar Centre / Dubai Mall, and Clock Tower Deira (VARA's own list groups the latter two as one entry). The activities covered are VA Advisory, Broker-Dealer, Custody, Exchange, Lending and Borrowing, and Management and Investment Services, plus VA Proprietary Trading, which separately requires a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from VARA rather than a full VA licence. The same notice restates that Legacy VASPs operating in Dubai before 7 February 2023 needed to submit applications for VARA oversight or guidance by 30 April 2023.
why_it_matters: This shows VARA, Dubai's dedicated virtual-asset regulator, working with a mainstream government service department to make applying for its virtual-asset licences physically accessible across Dubai -- useful context for readers unfamiliar with how Dubai's virtual-asset licensing regime, in force since February 2023, is administered day to day.

citations.quote: are now offered at DET branches
will require a No Objection Certificate [NoC] from VARA
